action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 28's local_eval.py reported LPIPS=0.5349 on validation objects using the current model.py/predict.py pipeline with fovy=30° — a sizable improvement over the last official test score of 0.5692. This suggests the rewritten model.py (iter 27) is more accurate than the version used in iter 23. We have 2 iterations and ~7.6 hours left. Analysis: Before committing to a fine-tuning run, we need to lock in an official test-set score with the current zero-shot checkpoint to confirm the improvement holds on test data and to establish a safe fallback. The model.py on disk should be complete (local_eval.py ran successfully), but I will do a 5-second import sanity check first. Decision: Run a quick syntax/import check, then immediately submit eval with checkpoints/baseline.pt. In iteration 30, if the score improves, we can optionally launch a short fine-tuning run with the remaining time.
action.command: python -c "from model import TripoSRWrapper; print('model OK')" && python -c "import torch; st=torch.load('checkpoints/baseline.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('ckpt OK, keys:', list(st.keys())[:5])"
